listOfQuestions.question: In Microbiology, what does "Bacterium" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A single-celled organism without a nucleus
wrongAnswers: An organism group that includes yeasts and molds
The ability of bacteria to survive an antibiotic
A chemical used to reduce microbes on surfaces

listOfQuestions.question: In Microbiology, what does "Virus" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: An infectious particle that replicates inside host cells
wrongAnswers: A single-celled eukaryotic microorganism
Microbes grown under controlled conditions
A staining method that helps classify bacteria

listOfQuestions.question: In Microbiology, what does "Fungus" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: An organism group that includes yeasts and molds
wrongAnswers: The community of microorganisms living in an environment
A shallow dish used to grow microorganisms
A visible group of microbes growing from cells

listOfQuestions.question: In Microbiology, what does "Protozoan" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A single-celled eukaryotic microorganism
wrongAnswers: A microbe or agent that can cause disease
Methods used to prevent unwanted contamination
Microbial metabolism that produces useful products without oxygen

listOfQuestions.question: In Microbiology, what does "Microbiome" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The community of microorganisms living in an environment
wrongAnswers: A drug used to treat bacterial infections
Destroying or removing all living microbes
A single-celled fungus often used in baking and brewing

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A microbe or agent that can cause disease
wrongAnswers: The ability of bacteria to survive an antibiotic
A chemical used to reduce microbes on surfaces
The protein coat around a virus

A staining method that helps classify bacteria
A cell used by a virus or microbe to reproduce
